{{Infobox character
| name = Valin
| aliases = Valin the Wanderer, Valin, the Wanderer
| abilities = {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|draw on Nye essence, speed of the Nye}}
| titles = {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[The Wanderer|the Wanderer]], [[Valinhall Incarnation|Incarnation of Valinhall]], [[Valinhall Incarnation]], [[Incarnation]], Overlord}}{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|, creator of Valinhall, founder of this Territory}}
| affiliations = {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|House Valinhall}}
| professions = {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|master}}{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|, [[Founder|founder]]}}
| residence = {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Valinhall]]}}
| appears_in = Traveler's Gate
| introduced = [[House of Blades (novel)|House of Blades]]
}}

''See also: [[Valin Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Valin, also known as the Wanderer or the [[Incarnation]] of [[Valinhall]], is the legendary founder of the [[Valinhall]] [[Territory]]. Originally a human, he arrived in [[the House]] through a sudden flash of light and sound, appearing to have been cast from another location. He eventually mastered [[the House]]'s many rooms and used his knowledge to create a [[gate]] connecting [[Valinhall]] to the human world. While some accounts suggest he may have been involved in a twisted experiment by [[Damasca]], others claim he turned the [[Territory]]'s power against [[Damasca]]. Valin is characterized by a striking and unsettling appearance, featuring solid black eyes with gleaming metallic silver irises, long hair or a beard filled with insects, and skin covered in black chains. He is a formidable combatant who favors speed and power, often wielding long swords of gold, silver, or [[steel]]. Though he once held the status of [[Incarnation]], he has described himself as a mere room guardian who grows weaker when no [[Travelers]] are present. Despite his capacity for intense rage and sudden violence, he has also acted as a mentor to others, teaching them about the unique nature of [[Valinhall]]'s power, which focuses on summoning strength into oneself rather than calling upon external forces.}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Valin]] named [[the House]] [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c9">''House of Blades'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Valin]] fashioned a key to open a Gate between his world and [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Valin]] wanted to stop human sacrifice before changing his mind.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c11">''House of Blades'', chapter 11</ref>}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Known as the Wanderer and the [[Incarnation]] of [[Valinhall]], Valin was the first [[Valinhall]] [[Traveler]] who forged the blade [[Mithra]] and killed [[King Zakareth the Fifth]]. After raising twelve students to bring down [[Damasca]], he was sealed under a [[Hanging Tree]] for twenty-five years following a fight with his former student, [[Kai]]. Now free, the [[Overlord]] has fallen and is covered in black chains that writhe like snakes across his skin. This threat, who possesses silver irises set in solid black eyes and moves with the speed of [[the Nye]], is intercepted by [[Simon]] and [[Indirial]]. During their confrontation, Valin fights [[Indirial]] to an even match and uses his gold-and-silver [[sword]], [[Dragon's Fang]], while [[Simon]] manages to slash the [[Incarnation]]'s forearm before falling upon Valin's blade.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] the Wanderer forged [[Mithra]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] the Wanderer killed [[King Zakareth the Fifth]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] has been sealed away for twenty-five years.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] is known as the Wanderer and the [[Incarnation]] of [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]]'s eyes have no whites and are dark with metallic silver irises.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] is a [[Valinhall Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] is [[Kai]]'s former master.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] is free.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] was the first [[Valinhall]] [[Traveler]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] has fallen.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] was the first of the group to fall.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] is an [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] and [[Indirial]] are tasked with intercepting [[Valin]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] is a man covered in the black chains of [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] is an [[Overlord]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] has eyes with solid black whites and gleaming [[steel]] colored parts.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] possesses silver eyes.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c11">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]]'s wound can knit itself whole with a bright green light.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] has chains on his skin that writhe like black snakes.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] uses a gold-and-silver [[sword]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] moves faster than [[Nye essence]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] possesses the speed of [[the Nye]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] is known as the Wanderer.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] uses a weapon called [[Dragon's Fang]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]]'s irises glow silver.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] is an [[Incarnation]] who fought [[Indirial]] evenly.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] is a looming threat to [[Simon]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] hates [[Talos]]'s father.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c16">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] raised twelve students to bring down [[Damasca]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c18">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] was sealed after a fight with [[Kai]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] was sealed under a [[Hanging Tree]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] is a person who spoke to [[Denner]] and [[Kai]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c19">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] uses the blade [[Mithra]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] is the [[Incarnation]] and has black-and-silver eyes.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] can draw on [[Nye essence]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] leaves a single slash across [[Valin]]’s forearm.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Valin]] has black eyes that gleam with metallic silver.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] aimed to make [[Valin]]'s head roll.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] fell on [[Valin]]'s [[sword]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22" />}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Known as the Wanderer, Valin is a former [[Incarnation]] and the founder of this [[Territory]] who previously died. As the creator of [[Valinhall]], he built the thirteenth bedroom and returned to [[the House]] as a deadly [[Incarnation]] driven by a personal vendetta or opposition to the sealing of [[Incarnations]]. He is characterized by gray eyes with hints of metallic silver that can transform into silver circles on pools of solid black, and he carries short swords—including a blade used in a three-part combination—and a dagger. Throughout his history, Valin has destroyed a village, attacked others to test their strength, and spoke of the joy of battle. Though [[King]] [[Zakareth]] could not control him, Valin eventually pronounced [[Simon]] fit for duty after the two had fought.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]] is the creator of [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c3">''City of Light'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]] carries short swords.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c5">''City of Light'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]] has a coil of smoke or shadow wrapped around his ankles like a chain.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]] is a former [[Incarnation]] and known as the Wanderer.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c6">''City of Light'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]] is the founder of this [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]]'s eyes can turn into silver circles on pools of solid black.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]] is a deadly [[Incarnation]] who has returned to [[the House]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c9">''City of Light'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]]'s questions are often in [[Simon]]'s thoughts.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10">''City of Light'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]] destroyed a village.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Simon]] has fought [[Valin]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11">''City of Light'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]] is a character [[Simon]] intends to ask questions.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c16">''City of Light'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]] carries a dagger.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]]'s eyes are gray with a hint of metallic silver.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]]'s [[sword]] can be used in a three-part combination.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]] is a character who has died before.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]] Incarnated due to a personal vendetta against [[Leah]]'s grandfather or opposition to the sealing of [[Incarnations]] under Hanging [[Trees]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c18">''City of Light'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] was unable to control [[Valin]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c19">''City of Light'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]] pronounced [[Simon]] fit for duty.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21">''City of Light'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]] did not like to call smoke.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]] attacked people to see if they were strong enough to handle it.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22">''City of Light'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]]'s blade is being carried by the enemy.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]], the Wanderer, is associated with the [[Dragon's Fang]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]] spoke of the joy of battle.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c23">''City of Light'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]] witnessed a double Avernus Incarnation.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]] built the thirteenth bedroom.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c28">''City of Light'', chapter 28</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|"When he claimed this House as his own, he gave it a name: Valin’s Hall. Valinhall."<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c9" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|"Using the books collected here, along with his own knowledge, he fashioned a key to open a Gate between his world—your world—and this one."<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c9" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Valin was the first of us," he said. "He was also the first to fall.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate characters]]
[[Category:Travelers]]
